description: Fix 29 failing CI tests — register DID command in CLI entry points and fix credential delete concurrent collision

userStories.title: Register DID command in CLI entry points
userStories.description: As a developer, I want `xrpl did` to be a valid CLI command so that all 28 failing DID tests can run.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `export { didCommand } from "./did.js";` to `src/commands/index.ts` (follow the same pattern as every other command in that file)
Add `program.addCommand(didCommand);` to `src/index.ts` (follow the same pattern as every other command in that file)
Import `didCommand` at the top of `src/index.ts` (destructured from `./commands/index.js`)
Running the CLI with `did --help` exits 0 and prints DID subcommand help
All DID tests pass: run `npx vitest run tests/e2e/did` and confirm 0 failures
userStories.notes: The ralph/did branch implemented src/commands/did.ts and its tests but forgot to wire it into index.ts. This is a 2-line fix — do NOT touch did.ts or any test files in this story.

userStories.title: Fix credential delete test concurrent collision
userStories.description: As a developer, I want each concurrent credential test to use a unique CredentialType so that post-delete assertions are not polluted by other in-flight tests.
acceptanceCriteria: Locate `tests/e2e/credential/` and identify every `it.concurrent` block that creates a credential on-chain

Each `it.concurrent` block uses a distinct `CredentialType` value — assign a unique ASCII string per test (e.g. `KYC_01`, `KYC_02`, etc.) and hex-encode with `Buffer.from('KYC_01').toString('hex').toUpperCase()`
The `credential delete > issuer deletes a credential they created` test filters `account_objects` by its own specific `CredentialType` when asserting the credential is gone
Run `npx vitest run tests/e2e/credential` and confirm 0 failures
userStories.notes: Root cause: concurrent tests share the same hardcoded CredentialType hex. After test A deletes its credential, test B's credential with the same type is still live, so the account_objects assertion finds it. Fix is to assign unique CredentialType strings per concurrent test block. Do NOT change command implementation — only test files.
